threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’ll cringe when you ask for a 'flat white' and the barista corrects you to 'white coffee'—definitely a newcomer mistake.
Caught the 333 last night, and a guy sang the entire 'Never Gonna Give You Up' at the back. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy confidently grilling sausages outside the U-Bahn station while a kid juggled oranges nearby. That was Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find guys playing chess in a U-Bahn station during a strike, completely unfazed by missing their trains.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they talk about the 'itch' of BART delays like it’s a seasonal allergy, not just a commute hassle.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo hop on the 19 bus at Civic Center, looking completely out of place next to a woman in pajamas. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just a series of cute squares. They miss the hidden street art in the alleys, like the one behind Carrer de Verdi.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still ask for a ‘café con leche’ instead of just a ‘café’. It’s the little things that give them away.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' instead of just 'the train' and look lost trying to navigate the River Lea paths.
Caught the 59 and a guy in a full tuxedo foot-tapping to his own playlist. Brixton's nightlife truly never sleeps, even on the buses.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw an old man feeding pigeons outside a closed bakery, while a hipster on a bike swerved around him, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a canal-side BBQ party with stray cats joining the picnic—locals and furballs bonding over grilled sausages.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they casually reference the BART delays like it’s weather gossip—'Did you hear the Civic Center’s closed again?'

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling in front of the Muni stop, only to trip over the crack in the sidewalk as the bus pulled up—classic San Francisco chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the ‘Barcelona chill’ is just about beaches; locals know it's about finding the best vermut on a Sunday afternoon at Plaça de la Vila.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new if they still look baffled trying to decipher the tram schedule at Diagonal. Real locals know it's all about the bus.
